ui_start_to_finish_status: one_click_local_launcher_available; open demo/final_demo_launcher.html from the local static QA server

demo_start_path.launcher: serve /private/tmp/v8_runtime_qa after npm install p5 three hydra-synth and open /demo/final_demo_launcher.html
demo_start_path.artifact_qa: serve /private/tmp/v8_runtime_qa after npm install p5 three hydra-synth and open /demo/golden_artifacts/browser_full_runtime_qa.html

global_fallback: Use demo/final_demo_launcher.html, demo/golden_demo_dataset.json, demo/demo_prompt_library.md, assets/preview_current.png, redacted/sanitized RAGAs evidence, and golden artifact QA without implying unavailable live components succeeded.

demos.prompt: Create a luminous audio-reactive Three.js scene for a capstone demo: concentric geometry, subtle bloom, FFT-driven motion accents, and a clear explanation of provider choice, execution mode, estimates, fallback, and escalation boundaries.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a Three.js creative-coding answer path and the generated Three.js artifact evidence.
demos.fallback_path: Use the generated Three.js artifact and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation or provider access fails.
demos.success_criteria: Real Three.js package renders the artifact nonblank in browser QA; no unsupported DCC/MCP or HoloMind claims.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: Three.js browser rendering passed through a temporary QA runtime; the app dependency graph was not changed.

demos.prompt: Create a p5.js sketch that turns golden-ratio circles and recursive polygons into a calm morphogenetic field. Include source-grounded runtime constraints, interaction notes, and a clear line between sacred-geometry inspiration and implemented browser code guidance.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a p5.js generative visual with explicit symbolic-claim boundaries.
demos.fallback_path: Use p5_sacred_geometry_sketch.js and browser full-runtime QA result if live generation fails.
demos.success_criteria: Real p5 package renders the artifact nonblank in browser QA; no metaphysical, medical, religious, or historical authority claim.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: The p5 artifact now uses real p5 runtime validation, not shim-only evidence.

demos.prompt: How do I build a glow-heavy kaleidoscopic browser visual with shaders or post-processing passes? Give source-grounded runtime tradeoffs, failure risks, and fallback implementation options.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows shader/post-processing guidance and the generated GLSL artifact.
demos.fallback_path: Use glsl_kaleidoscope_field.frag and WebGL QA evidence if live preview is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: GLSL compiles, links, draws, and pixel-checks nonblank in local WebGL; Hydra synth artifact also renders nonblank through real hydra-synth QA.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: GLSL compiles directly in WebGL, and Hydra is now bounded to a real local hydra-synth browser artifact path.

demos.prompt: Answer a creative-coding runtime question with registered source grounding and explain which retrieved sources shaped the response.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows retrieval-grounded guidance and explains source boundaries.
demos.fallback_path: Use redacted latest-live RAGAs, sanitized RAGAs result rows, and retrieval smoke evidence if live retrieval is unavailable.
demos.success_criteria: Local Chroma retrieval returns results for 7/7 capstone scenarios; redacted latest-live RAGAs has 4 rows, 0 skipped, 0 metric failures across context_precision, faithfulness, and answer_relevancy.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: Raw private live-session text stays local, while the redacted latest-live fixture now has provider-backed RAGAs metrics.

demos.prompt: Turn a concentric mandala motif into a practical browser visual system with motion, rhythm, runtime choices, source-grounded constraints, and a clear boundary between symbolic inspiration and operational creative-coding guidance.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows symbolic intent translated into operational visual structure.
demos.fallback_path: Use the p5 artifact, symbolic prompt, and claim-safety explanation.
demos.success_criteria: Symbolic language remains aesthetic/operational; p5 artifact renders nonblank through real p5 QA.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: The demo converts symbolic language into browser implementation guidance without authority claims.

demos.prompt: Translate a basilica axis, mandala grid, and gallery circulation constraint into a browser-based installation concept. Include geometry, audience movement, preview strategy, and boundaries: no LIDAR, photogrammetry, engineering certification, or historical authority claims.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows installation planning, geometry, audience movement, and boundary language.
demos.fallback_path: Use demo prompt library and offline walkthrough; no engineering certification or reconstruction claim.
demos.success_criteria: Planning output is reviewable and bounded; no real-world reconstruction or certification claim.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: This is planning guidance, not built-environment authority or a DCC execution claim.

demos.prompt: Build a mythopoetic narrative arc for an audiovisual sketch: threshold, transformation, return, visual motifs, audio rhythm, and audience explanation. Keep it as creative storytelling guidance, not religious, psychological, medical, or metaphysical truth.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows narrative-to-immersive-scene guidance with ethics framing.
demos.fallback_path: Use prompt library, ethics doc, and generated visual artifacts to illustrate the scene path.
demos.success_criteria: Narrative remains creative storytelling guidance and avoids therapy, doctrine, or metaphysical truth claims.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: This flow demonstrates creative direction and safety framing rather than claiming objective symbolic truth.

demos.prompt: Plan a HoloGenesis-style creative project bundle for a browser installation: concept, runtimes, retrieval needs, preview plan, artifact package, evaluation checks, fallback route, and handoff boundaries. Do not claim HoloMind, HOLOiVERSE, external DCC execution, or autonomous delivery.
demos.expected_behavior: Presenter shows a project-bundle planning handoff with explicit future-scope boundaries.
demos.fallback_path: Use the offline dataset and scorecard boundaries; do not claim HoloMind, HOLOiVERSE, live DCC/MCP, or autonomous delivery.
demos.success_criteria: Planner output is bounded as project planning and handoff metadata only.
demos.reviewer_talking_point: HoloGenesis is a bounded planner/demo prompt, not an implemented HoloMind or HOLOiVERSE runtime.
